meta.title: 四字熟語・ことわざ入門
meta.description: Introduction to common four-character idioms and proverbs.

explanation.en: 一石二鳥 = killing two birds with one stone (achieving two goals with one action).
explanation.ko: 一石二鳥 = 일석이조(한 가지 행동으로 두 가지 목표 달성).

questions.prompt: 「一石二鳥（いっせきにちょう）」の意味は？
choices: killing two birds with one stone

explanation.en: 四苦八苦 = struggling greatly, going through great difficulty.
explanation.ko: 四苦八苦 = 매우 고생하다, 큰 어려움을 겪다.

questions.prompt: 「四苦八苦（しくはっく）」の意味は？

explanation.en: 一生懸命 = doing something with all one's might / very hard.
explanation.ko: 一生懸命 = 최선을 다해/매우 열심히.

questions.prompt: 「一生懸命（いっしょうけんめい）」の意味は？
choices: doing one's best with all one's effort

explanation.en: いっしょうけんめいべんきょうしました = studied with all effort.
explanation.ko: いっしょうけんめいべんきょうしました = 열심히 공부했습니다.

questions.prompt: 「かれは いっしょうけんめい ＿＿。」(He studied with all his effort.)

explanation.en: 猿も木から落ちる = even monkeys fall from trees — even experts make mistakes.
explanation.ko: 猿も木から落ちる = 원숭이도 나무에서 떨어진다 — 전문가도 실수할 때가 있다.

questions.prompt: 「猿も木から落ちる（さるもきからおちる）」の意味は？

explanation.en: 七転び八起き = fall seven times, rise eight — perseverance despite repeated failure.
explanation.ko: 七転び八起き = 칠전팔기 — 반복된 실패에도 불구한 끈기.

questions.prompt: 「七転び八起き（ななころびやおき）」の意味は？
choices: falling down seven times, getting up eight (perseverance)

explanation.en: 石の上にも三年 = even a cold stone becomes warm after sitting on it for 3 years — patience pays off.
explanation.ko: 石の上にも三年 = 차가운 돌도 3년 앉아 있으면 따뜻해진다 — 인내가 결실을 맺는다.

questions.prompt: 「石の上にも三年（いしのうえにもさんねん）」の意味は？
choices: patience and perseverance bring success (even sitting on a cold stone for 3 years warms it)

explanation.en: がんばりましょう = let's keep doing our best. Following the proverb about patience.
explanation.ko: がんばりましょう = 힘냅시다. 인내에 관한 속담을 따라서.

questions.prompt: 「やめたく なっても、いしのうえにも さんねん と いうから、もう すこし ＿＿。」(Even if you want to quit, since they say patience pays off, let's keep going a bit longer.)

explanation.en: 百聞は一見にしかず = seeing once is worth more than hearing a hundred times — seeing is believing.
explanation.ko: 百聞は一見にしかず = 백 번 듣는 것보다 한 번 보는 것이 낫다 — 백문이 불여일견.

questions.prompt: 「百聞は一見にしかず（ひゃくぶんはいっけんにしかず）」の意味は？
choices: seeing once is better than hearing a hundred times

explanation.en: 急がば回れ = if you're in a hurry, take the roundabout (safe) way — more haste, less speed.
explanation.ko: 急がば回れ = 급할수록 돌아가라 — 서두르면 일을 그르친다.

questions.prompt: 「急がば回れ（いそがばまわれ）」の意味は？
choices: if you're in a hurry, take the safe longer route (haste makes waste)

explanation.en: 十人十色 = ten people, ten colours — everyone has different tastes/opinions.
explanation.ko: 十人十色 = 열 명이면 열 가지 색 — 사람마다 다르다.

questions.prompt: 「十人十色（じゅうにんといろ）」の意味は？
choices: ten people, ten different preferences/opinions (everyone is different)

explanation.en: じゅうにんといろですね = as they say, everyone is different (agreement expression).
explanation.ko: じゅうにんといろですね = 그야말로 십인십색이군요(동의 표현).

questions.prompt: 「しゅみは ひとに よって ちがいます。じゅうにんといろ ＿＿。」(Hobbies differ from person to person. As they say, 'ten people, ten colours.')

explanation.en: 自業自得 = reaping the consequences of one's own actions (usually negative outcomes deserved).
explanation.ko: 自業自得 = 자업자득(보통 마땅한 부정적 결과).

questions.prompt: 「自業自得（じごうじとく）」の意味は？
choices: reaping what you sow / getting what you deserve (often negative)

explanation.en: 弱肉強食 = the weak become the food of the strong — survival of the fittest.
explanation.ko: 弱肉強食 = 약육강식 — 적자생존.

questions.prompt: 「弱肉強食（じゃくにくきょうしょく）」の意味は？
choices: the weak are prey to the strong / survival of the fittest

explanation.en: 百聞は一見にしかず。= Seeing once is worth more than hearing a hundred times.
explanation.ko: 百聞は一見にしかず。= 백문이 불여일견.

questions.prompt: 正しい順番に並べてください。
questions.answer: 百聞 は 一見 に しかず。

explanation.en: Match idioms/proverbs to their meanings.
explanation.ko: 사자성어/속담과 그 의미를 연결하세요.

questions.prompt: 四字熟語・ことわざとその意味をむすびましょう。
pairs: killing two birds with one stone

explanation.en: じごうじとく = just deserts / reaping what was sown.
explanation.ko: じごうじとく = 자업자득.

questions.prompt: 「あんなに わるいことを したのだから、しごとを くびに なったのは ＿＿ です。」(Since he did such bad things, getting fired was just deserts.)

explanation.en: 一石二鳥 means hitting TWO birds (二鳥), not one. The explanation is incorrect.
explanation.ko: 一石二鳥은 한 마리가 아닌 두 마리(二鳥)의 새를 맞추는 것을 의미합니다.

questions.prompt: まちがいを見つけてください：「いっせきにちょうとは、一つの石で一羽の鳥をとることです。」
choices: 一羽 → 二羽（二鳥という意味）

explanation.en: しくはっく = struggling greatly (great hardship). Apt for the difficulty of working and studying simultaneously.
explanation.ko: しくはっく = 매우 고생함. 일과 공부를 동시에 하는 어려움에 적합.

questions.prompt: 「べんきょうしながら はたらくのは ＿＿ です。とても たいへんです。」(Studying while working is a real struggle.)

explanation.en: 七転び八起きのせいしん = the spirit of perseverance despite repeated failure.
explanation.ko: 七転び八起きのせいしん = 반복된 실패에도 불구한 끈기의 정신.

questions.prompt: 「彼は七転び八起きの精神で頑張った。」の意味は？
choices: He worked hard with the spirit of never giving up despite failures.
He fell seven times and stopped trying.
He succeeded on his first try.

explanation.en: れい (例) = example. じゅうにんといろのれい = an example of 'everyone being different'.
explanation.ko: れい(예) = 예시. じゅうにんといろのれい = '십인십색'의 예시.

questions.prompt: 「かのじょは いつも じぶんの いけんを いう。 まさに じゅうにんといろの ＿＿ だ。」(She always voices her own opinion. It's exactly an example of 'everyone is different.')

explanation.en: Learning idioms and proverbs deepens cultural understanding and enables more natural, sophisticated expression.
explanation.ko: 사자성어와 속담을 배우는 것은 문화적 이해를 깊게 하고 더 자연스럽고 정교한 표현을 가능하게 합니다.

questions.prompt: B1レベルで四字熟語やことわざを学ぶ意義は？
choices: より深い日本文化理解と自然な表現力の向上
文法テストのためだけ
